prediction.summary: Multiple failed SSH login attempts were observed from a single source IP address (203.0.113.45) targeting various user accounts. A total of 11 distinct user accounts experienced failed login attempts within a short timeframe, suggesting a password spraying or brute-force attack. The connection from the suspicious IP was subsequently closed.

recommendations: Immediately block the source IP address 203.0.113.45 at the firewall or host level to prevent further attempts.

Investigate if any of the targeted user accounts (alice, bob, carol, dave, eve, frank, grace, henry, iris, jack, kate) have experienced successful logins from any unusual IP addresses or at unusual times.
Review SSH server logs for any other suspicious activity or successful logins around the time of the attack.
Ensure SSH is configured to use strong authentication methods (e.g., key-based authentication) and disable password authentication where possible.
Implement or review rate-limiting mechanisms (e.g., fail2ban) on SSH to automatically block IPs after a few failed attempts.
Consider implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all SSH access.
